steps.title: Identify the exponential form
steps.content: The function $f(t) = 28{,}000(2)^t$ has the standard exponential form $f(t) = a \cdot b^t$, where $a = 28{,}000$ is the initial value (output at $t = 0$) and $b = 2$ is the growth factor (the multiplier each hour).

steps.title: Evaluate at the initial measurement
steps.content:
Substitute $t = 0$:
$$f(0) = 28{,}000 \cdot 2^0 = 28{,}000 \cdot 1 = 28{,}000$$
Since $t$ is measured in hours after the initial measurement, $t = 0$ is the initial measurement. So $28{,}000$ represents the yeast cell count at that starting moment.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — The number of yeast cells in the population at the initial measurement: matches $f(0) = 28{,}000$. Correct.
  • Choice B — The number of hours it takes for the number of yeast cells in the population to double: that is determined by the base $b = 2$, not by $28{,}000$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — The number of yeast cells in the population 1 hour after the initial measurement: that would be $f(1) = 28{,}000 \cdot 2 = 56{,}000$, not $28{,}000$. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — The number of times the number of yeast cells in the population doubles in 1 hour: the population doubles once per hour, not $28{,}000$ times. Incorrect.
That confirms A is correct.
